title: Agent Pulse
description: Source-code motion for curated AI-agent and agent-adjacent projects covered by The Git Reporter.

scope.coverageStatement: V0.1 measures visible source-code motion, hardening proxy signals, daily git trends, and The Git Reporter article coverage only. Community feedback metrics are absent until the GitHub feedback collector ships.

editorialRead.headline: Code motion is visible; community health is still the missing half.
editorialRead.dek: Today Agent Pulse tracks 10 watched projects through public git motion, source receipts, and The Git Reporter coverage. Treat it as a reported signal board, not a definitive market ranking.
bullets: openclaw/openclaw is still the loudest source-code motion signal with 1,175 commits in seven days.
anomalyco/opencode is the strongest baseline mover, 97.2 commits above its 30-day pace.
openclaw/openclaw leads the hardening proxy, but that proxy is built from commit-subject evidence, not release quality.
openai/codex has the deepest article trail on The Git Reporter, with 62 linked articles.
openclaw/openclaw cooled the most week-over-week, down 1,206 commits from the previous seven days.
editorialRead.watchNext: The next meaningful upgrade is feedback flow: issues opened and closed, review latency, stale backlog, and the share of work that starts outside the maintainer team.
editorialRead.caveat: V0.1 does not collect GitHub issue flow, PR review timing, Discord, X/Twitter, Reddit, Hacker News, or private backlog data.
